_comment: Crawl order. Sources are grouped into 4 phases. The skill processes phase 1 first; when all sources of a phase are completed it escalates to the next. Reaching the end of phase 4 is NOT 'done' — it is the trigger to start CREATIVE DISCOVERY (SKILL.md): the skill invents fresh search angles and keeps going. coop-hunter is eternal; only Ctrl+C (or the one-time reeval_only migration) stops it.

sources.note: Highest yield. Steam-explicit signal for finite co-op content.
Arcade mode — finite levels without strong story. Maps to endingType 'levels' or 'arcade-goal'.
Co-Optimus official Steam curator. Pre-curated quality.
Games with Main Story length filled = guaranteed finite.
Community-marked completed co-op games. Strong signal that game has an ending.
Top posts of all time. Extract game names from titles and link cards.

PRIMARY phase-4 engine — proven highest-yield (5-11 net-new per page across offsets 250-450 on 2026-06-06). Walks the LONG TAIL of Steam's Co-op category (~13k apps) by review count, deep past the saturated popular front (first ~300 are all catalogued). Cursor = the `start` offset stored in progress.json: RESUME from where the last run stopped, page +50, do NOT reinvent it ad-hoc as a one-off WebSearch (that regression is exactly why 2026-06-27 inline bursts found 0 — they fell back to saturated listicles instead of resuming this crawl). Exhausted only when start >= total_count OR several consecutive pages yield 0 net-new AFTER exclusion. PARALLELIZE with subagents when available: give each agent a disjoint offset slice (a:450-650, b:650-850, ...) — that parallel tail-mining is what made the subagent sweep outperform inline. Response is JSON; appids are in results_html as data-ds-appid. Dedup every appid vs data.js + reeval.tsv + hard-block.tsv BEFORE any per-candidate work.

Re-process state/skipped.tsv with strictest rules. If a game was skipped as 'ambiguous' or 'unclear_ending', re-evaluate with fresh data. If it now passes, add it. Most entries should still be skipped.

For each of the top-25 highest-rated entries in data.js (rating >=90), fetch Steam's 'More like this' / 'Similar by tags' recommendations. Extract new candidates, dedupe, process each through the standard procedure.

queries: best co-op puzzle games PC with ending 2026
co-op horror games PC finite campaign steam
co-op JRPG steam two player ending
co-op platformer two player PC steam
couch co-op story games PC finite
remote play together best co-op games
sources.note: WebSearch for niche queries that might surface candidates not in any structured source.
Drill source: Backloggd, ordered by user-count instead of rating — surfaces less-discussed games that nonetheless people actually finished.
Drill source: community discussions about co-op games. Posts often list 5-10 games each. Extract names, dedupe heavily.

queries: co-op games to play with friend 2024 playlist
best 2 player PC games with ending youtube playlist
hidden gem co-op story games PC
co-op survival games with ending steam
co-op puzzle games like Portal 2 steam
sources.note: Drill source: niche WebSearch surfacing games NOT in mainstream lists. Parse curator playlist titles + descriptions for game names.
Drill source: Reddit threads where people explicitly say they finished a co-op game = strong endless-filter signal.
Drill source: Wikipedia's curated lists of co-op games. Often surfaces older / obscure titles with verified ending status.

_method_notes.steamdb_tag: SteamDB pages are HTML. Use WebFetch to extract game names + appids from the table rows. FALLBACK (proven): SteamDB often returns HTTP 403 to bots — when that happens, use the Steam Store search by tag instead, e.g. https://store.steampowered.com/search/?tags=<tag_id>&category2=9&supportedlang=english&os=win&sort_by=Reviews_DESC&page={N} (category2=9 = co-op).
_method_notes.co_optimus: Each row has a game name + link. From individual game page, extract co-op modes + supported player counts + 'Campaign Length' field if present.
_method_notes.curator: Steam curator pages. Extract game cards (name + Steam app link).
_method_notes.reddit_top_posts: JSON API. Parse 'data.children' for posts; extract game mentions.
_method_notes.webfetch: Generic article scrape. Look for game lists with links to Steam.
_method_notes.reeval_skipped: Read state/skipped.tsv. For each row where reason is in [ambiguous, unclear_ending, not_enough_reviews], run validate() again. If now passes, build entry and append.
_method_notes.steam_more_like_this: Use Steam's tag-similarity to expand. Each top entry's Steam page has 'More like this' carousel. Scrape those titles.
_method_notes.websearch_queries: Run each query through WebSearch. Parse results for game names mentioned. Dedupe heavily against existing data.js. NOTE: on a mature catalog this method is SATURATED — listicles/roundups only surface already-known popular games (proven 0-yield on 2026-06-27). Prefer steam_coop_deep_scan; use websearch_queries only for variety, not as the main engine.
_method_notes.steam_coop_deep_scan: GET the url_template with {start} = the stored offset (JSON response; send header User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0). Parse results_html for data-ds-appid attributes -> appids. For each NEW appid (not already in data.js / reeval.tsv / hard-block.tsv), run the standard Per-candidate procedure. After the page, advance the offset by page_size (50) and PERSIST it to progress.json (current_offset) so the next burst resumes deeper instead of restarting. total_count is in the JSON response — keep paging until start >= total_count. This is the Steam-store search by the Co-op category (category2=9) sorted by review count; it was the fallback for steamdb_tag 403s, now promoted to a first-class RESUMABLE source because it is the catalog's highest-yield discovery method (the popular front is exhausted; net-new lives in the deep tail).
